issues: blade proportionally long relative to handle/guard—silhouette reads more sword than dagger
grip detail density slightly busy at 1× zoom, loses charm at small size
cyan blade highlight could use one brighter specular pixel for more magical pop
reasoning: Emotional (60%): The saturated teal/cyan blade with red grip accents is strongly Zelda-coded—bright, magical, warm, not threatening. Color palette sits comfortably in the Hyrule toy-diorama mood. Silhouette is legible and friendly. Loses points because the blade-to-handle length ratio skews toward shortsword, weakening the 'dagger' read and slightly diluting the charming compact-weapon archetype. Score ~7/10 emotional. Technical (40%): Clean 1px hard outlines throughout, visible at all zoom panels. Hard shading in 2-3 tones on blade (dark teal / mid teal / light teal) and handle (dark gray / mid gray). No anti-aliasing detected. Pixels are grid-snapped. Compares favorably to Denzi reference in outline quality and palette discipline. Minor: grip section has slightly more color events than Denzi peers at equivalent scale. Score ~7/10 technical. Weighted: 0.6×7 + 0.4×7 = 7. Marginal—usable with minor proportion tweak.
